description: Unified execution logic for the ADA Decision Engine. Defines module execution order, the is_qualified_individual composite (WR-1 through WR-5), employer_compliance_status derivation, and ada_covered derivation. Does not re-implement module logic. The wrapper's only computational responsibility is the WR composite and the summary output aggregation rules defined below.
execution_order_rationale: Module 1 must run first to establish coverage and produce preliminary_qualified_without_accommodation. Module 2 runs second because it depends on Module 1 outputs and produces accommodation_required, which is required by the WR composite. The WR composite runs third because Module 3 and Module 4 both consume the final is_qualified_individual value. Module 3 runs fourth. Module 4 runs fifth because it consumes outputs from all three prior modules.

is_qualified_individual_composite.description: Final composite determination of whether the individual is a qualified individual with a disability. Computed by the wrapper between Module 2 and Module 3. Consumes preliminary_qualified_without_accommodation (Module 1 component 1.4b), accommodation_required (Module 2 component 2.4), accommodation_theoretically_effective (wrapper input), and denial_basis (Module 2 component 2.4). Rules are evaluated in strict priority order; the first matching rule fires and no subsequent rules are evaluated.

rules.label: Qualified without accommodation
rules.reasoning_template: The individual can perform the essential functions of the position without any accommodation. Module 2 outcome does not affect qualified individual status. Qualified status is established under the primary definition.

rules.label: Qualified with accommodation
rules.condition: preliminary_qualified_without_accommodation = no AND accommodation_required = yes AND accommodation_theoretically_effective = yes
rules.reasoning_template: The individual cannot perform essential functions without accommodation but can do so with an effective accommodation. Accommodation is required and would be effective. Qualified individual status is established with accommodation under the alternative definition.

rules.label: Not qualified — accommodation legally unavailable
rules.condition: preliminary_qualified_without_accommodation = no AND accommodation_required = no AND (denial_basis = undue_hardship OR denial_basis = direct_threat)
rules.reasoning_template: The individual cannot perform essential functions without accommodation, and no accommodation can be required because the employer has established a legitimate defense ({denial_basis}). No effective accommodation exists or can be legally compelled. Qualified individual status is not established.

rules.label: Indeterminate — accommodation outcome unresolved
rules.reasoning_template: The individual cannot perform essential functions without accommodation. Whether the individual is qualified therefore depends on whether accommodation can be required, which cannot be determined on the available facts. Qualified individual status is indeterminate pending resolution of the accommodation analysis.

rules.label: Indeterminate — upstream input unresolved (catch-all)
rules.condition: No prior WR rule has fired AND any upstream input is indeterminate
rules.reasoning_template: An upstream indeterminate value prevents resolution of the qualified individual composite. The specific unresolved input is captured in indeterminate_factors. Qualified individual status cannot be determined until the upstream uncertainty is resolved.

ada_covered.logic: ada_covered = true if and only if is_covered_employer = true AND is_covered_individual = true. If either is false, ada_covered = false and downstream modules short-circuit.

has_qualifying_disability.logic: pass-through from has_qualifying_disability output of component 1.3. No transformation applied.

is_qualified_individual.logic: Computed per is_qualified_individual_composite rules above. This is the final authoritative value. The Module 1 preliminary value is not surfaced in the summary outputs.

accommodation_required.logic: pass-through from accommodation_required output of component 2.4. No transformation applied.

logic.compliant: interactive_process_compliant = yes AND inquiry_permitted = yes (for all stages evaluated) AND confidentiality_compliant = yes
logic.non_compliant: interactive_process_compliant = no OR inquiry_permitted = no (for any stage evaluated) OR confidentiality_compliant = no
logic.indeterminate: no component produces no AND at least one of (interactive_process_compliant, inquiry_permitted, confidentiality_compliant) produces indeterminate
logic.evaluation_note: non_compliant takes precedence over indeterminate. If any component produces no, the status is non_compliant regardless of whether other components are indeterminate.

violation_risk_level.logic: pass-through from overall_risk_level output of component 4.5. No transformation applied.

recommended_actions.logic: pass-through from recommended_actions output of component 4.5. Empty array [] if no recommendations are generated.

indeterminate_factors.source: all module components that produced indeterminate determinations
indeterminate_factors.logic: For each module component across all four modules: if the component produced an indeterminate determination, collect its key_uncertainties array. Add entry to indeterminate_factors with key '{module_id}.{component_id}' and value = key_uncertainties array. Return empty object {} if no component produced indeterminate. Key format must use the module_id exactly as defined in module metadata and the component_name (not component_id number) from module schemas.

module_outputs.logic: Full structured output from each module, keyed by module_id string. Keys: ada_coverage_eligibility, ada_accommodation_analysis, ada_employer_obligations, ada_violation_risk. Value for each key is the complete module output object as returned by that module's execution.
